title: Investment Proposal Drafting Agent
problem: Every bake-off, DDQ, and consultant questionnaire pulls the same team away from clients to reconstruct answers that already exist somewhere in a past PowerPoint or ADV filing.
solution: Agent classifies the incoming RFP/DDQ, searches past pitches, ADV filings, and firm narrative archives, drafts tone-unified sections with regulatory disclosures, and routes to the lead advisor for final review.
whoItsFor: Sales, BD, and IR teams at RIAs
workflowSteps: Incoming RFP/DDQ/bake-off request classified by request type
Agent searches: past pitch books, ADV filings, DDQ archives, firm narrative docs
Drafts each section matching house style and regulatory disclosure requirements (Form ADV, CRS)
Reviewer receives draft with source references for every section
Lead advisor approves, edits, and sends to prospect
keyInsights: RAG over 'past successful responses' is the core value pattern here. The key risk is that retrieved language may be from a deprecated filing — freshness metadata on every chunk and a 'last approved for use' date on firm narrative documents is essential.

relatedExams.why: Prompt and Context Engineering — style constraints, compliance language
Solution Design & Architecture — RAG over proprietary document archives

mermaidDiagram:
flowchart LR
  A([RFP or DDQ Input]) --> B[Classify Request Type]
  B --> C[Search Archives]
  C --> D1[Past Pitch Books]
  C --> D2[ADV Filings]
  C --> D3[DDQ Archives]
  D1 & D2 & D3 --> E[Draft Sections: House Style + Disclosures]
  E --> F([Lead Advisor Review])
architectureNotes: The agent takes client profile data (risk tolerance, horizon, objectives) and pulls relevant market data and fund performance metrics from internal APIs. A portfolio-fit model scores available products against the client profile. The drafting LLM produces a personalised proposal narrative; a compliance check step validates suitability statements and required disclosures before human RM sign-off.

tools: Rules-based suitability engine (MiFID II / Reg BI)

failureModes.mode: Proposal recommends product outside client risk tolerance
failureModes.mitigation: Hard constraint layer pre-filters product universe by risk band BEFORE LLM drafts prose; compliance rule engine validates final recommendation independently.

failureModes.mode: Required regulatory disclosures missing from final proposal
failureModes.mitigation: Disclosure checklist applied post-generation; each required disclosure has a presence check; proposal blocked from delivery if any disclosure absent.

failureModes.mode: Portfolio return figures use wrong benchmark
failureModes.mitigation: Benchmark selection is a deterministic rule (client geography × asset class → benchmark ID); LLM receives pre-computed figures, never calculates returns independently.

scalingConsiderations: Relationship manager productivity: typical RM spends 4–6 hours on a proposal; agent reduces to 30-minute review + sign-off — enables 5× more proposal throughput.
Regulatory variation: MiFID II (EU), Reg BI (US), MAS FAA (Singapore) have different disclosure requirements; parameterise by jurisdiction.
Proposal versioning: client may request multiple scenario variations (conservative / moderate / aggressive); agent generates all three in parallel for single review session.

integrations.note: Client profile, risk tolerance, investment objectives
Historical performance, current holdings, benchmark data
Approved investment products and their suitability profiles
Sends proposal for client and RM e-signature
Archives signed proposal with version history
